title: Language-Specific Applications
text: Language-Specific Applications In a language specific Uniface, you can develop an application entirely in that language. The Uniface IDE has an English user interface, even if you installed Uniface with a non-English character set. However, you can enter the non-English characters in labels, menu items, and so on to develop an application completely in that language. Figure 1. Simplified Chinese Application in Development (Uniface 9) Figure 2. Deployed Simplified Chinese Application In a Uniface version installed with a specific character set, there are some restrictions on where the characters can be used. In general, text that is visible to the user of your application can be in the specific language, for example in labels, menu items, ValRep lists, and so on. Most object names can only use ASCII characters. For more information, see Restrictions on Use of Unicode and Installable Character Sets . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
